title: The chart's silences — flows known to history that the flow matrix does not (or cannot) quantify
note: PLAN-3 §1 rule 5: a known trade is never silently zero, and a silence we cannot answer is recorded HERE, with its reason and our chosen treatment, instead of being dropped. treatment=asserted means the silence was answered — `pointer` names the system carrying our stated estimate; gestured means acknowledged, and a candidate for ambient content (S3) without volume claims; excluded means out of scope, with the reason on record. Basin authoring (R3) appends to this register; S3 renders it as a reference page.

silences.note: The ships rankings count foreign-going clearances (R1 decision), so Europe's single largest generator of ship movements is invisible to them. Answered: the flow matrix carries a reconstructed collier system (~150 voyages/yr in the 1550s → ~4,000–6,500/yr by 1810).

Coasting dwarfed foreign-going traffic in vessel numbers everywhere — grain hoys, wine barks, fish carriers, timber floats. No source counts it; no honest range exists at world scale. Acknowledged as the sea's ambient metabolism; candidate for ambient rendering (S3) rather than a volume claim.

Hundreds of herring busses at sea every season — among the largest fleets afloat, but grounds-traffic rather than port-to-port trade, so it fits no lane. Candidate ambient feature; the fishery's SIZE is well documented even though it has no origin→destination shape.

The anchoring series is itself distorted: under-declaration, wartime neutral re-flagging, and the Swedish exemption (1645–1710) all bias the registers the 'counted' systems rest on. Carried as a caveat on evidence=counted rather than as a flow — the register records that even the archive's firmest ground is soft.
Boats below burden thresholds — wherries, prams, coasters under ~20 tons — were never registered anywhere. Below the simulation's vessel scale; excluded knowingly.

silences.scope: Atlantic · Mediterranean · Indian Ocean · SE Asia · East Asia
silences.note: Known entries awaiting basin authoring (R3): Caribbean inter-island smuggling (silent BY EVASION — unrecorded because recording was the danger); the Nanyang junk trade's under-registration in European sources; African coastwise and lagoon trade; Indian Ocean country trade beyond company books; Pacific islander voyaging (real, seaborne, and outside every European series). Each becomes asserted-or-gestured, with reasoning, as its basin is authored.
